title: hydrangea borealis
intro: Hydrangea borealis, also known as the mountain hydrangea, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its white or pale pink flowers and broad, lobed leaves. Traditionally, the roots of this plant have been used in herbal medicine, valued for their medicinal properties, including their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments. The health benefits of Hydrangea borealis are numerous, with its use having been associated with reducing pain and inflammation, as well as aiding in the treatment of kidney stones and other urinary issues. In herbal preparations, the roots of Hydrangea borealis are typically dried and used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
